div1.entryFree:
Consign, 1) intr. to agree, to come to the same terms: God --ing to my good intents, H4B V, 2, 143. we'll c. thereto, H5 V, 2, 90. H5 V, 2, 90 all lovers must c. to thee and come to dust, Cymb. IV, 2, 275 (== come to the same state, submit to the same terms).
2) to assign, to allot: as many farewells as be stars in heaven, with distinct breath and --ed kisses to them, Troil. IV, 4, 47 (== kisses allotted to them). -- In H4B IV, 1, 175 some M. Edd. consigned, O. Edd. confined, q. v.
